channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Schumer accuses Trump of 'skipping town' during shutdown as president heads to Asia for trade talks
item.description: Sen. Chuck Schumer blasts President Donald Trump for leaving the United States for Asia during the government shutdown, accusing the president of "skipping town.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Poway Councilmember Tony Blain charged with four felonies days before recall election
item.description: The San Diego County District Attorney’s office has filed five charges, including four felonies, against embattled Poway City Councilmember Tony Blain.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Franco, Fox 5

item.title: Newsom bails out Planned Parenthood with $140M to keep 100 clinics open after Trump cuts
item.description: California governor Gavin Newsom announces $140 million in state funding to keep 109 Planned Parenthood clinics open amid federal cuts and defunding efforts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US imposes sanctions on Colombia’s president and family members over drug trade allegations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration imposed sanctions Friday on Colombian President Gustavo Petro, his family and a member of his government over accusations of involvement in the global drug trade, sharply escalating tensions with the leftist leader of one of the closest U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In emotional speech, Zohran Mamdani defends Muslim identity against 'racist and baseless' attacks
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Zohran Mamdani, the Democratic nominee for New York City mayor, pledged Friday to further embrace his Muslim identity in response to growing attacks by former Gov. Andrew Cuomo and his surrogates that he characterized as “racist and baseless.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reagan Foundation becomes the latest US institution drawn into Donald Trump's controversies
item.description: The Ronald Reagan Presidential Foundation and Institute is drawing blowback and praise for arguing that a Canadian government advertisement playing in U.S. markets misrepresented the 40th president’s words to blast President Donald Trump’s tariff policies.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow And Thalia Beaty, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump administration won't tap contingency fund to keep food aid flowing, memo says
item.description: The Trump administration says it won't use a roughly $5 billion contingency fund to keep food aid flowing in November amid the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The CEO of the Alamo's historic site has resigned after a top Texas Republican criticized her
item.description: The CEO of the nonprofit managing the Alamo resigned after a powerful Republican state official criticized her publicly, suggesting that her views aren't compatible with the history of the Texas shrine. Kate Rogers said in a statement Friday that she had resigned the day before, after Lt. Gov.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Intel Democrats press Gabbard on Trump administration boat strikes
item.description: Leading Intelligence Committee Democrats are accusing the Trump administration of leaving them in the dark on strikes in the Caribbean that have killed more than 40 people, demanding information about the intelligence community’s role.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida lawmaker revives E-Verify bill for stronger enforcement
item.description: tate lawmakers kicked off last year with not one but three special sessions on illegal immigration. A key debate then was E-Verify, which has now made its return to the statehouse.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NYC early voting 2025: When locations open and how to find them
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) -- Election Day is right around the corner, but to help residents beat the long lines, early voting kicks off in New York City this weekend. Early voting begins on Saturday and runs through Nov. 2.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, PIX 11

item.title: Trump says a Canadian ad misstated Ronald Reagan's views on tariffs. Here are the facts and context
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump pulled out of trade talks with Canada Thursday night, furious over what he called a “fake’’ television ad from Ontario’s provincial government that quoted former U.S.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US sanctions Colombian president and family over drug trafficking allegations
item.description: Treasury Department accuses Colombian President Petro of enabling cocaine production surge while he calls sanctions a "complete paradox" in response.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump to meet Chinese president next week for key trade deal: White House
item.description: President Trump is preparing for a high-stakes trip to Asia aimed at testing his reputation as a dealmaker, with a potential trade agreement with China on the line.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG and PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: DOJ prepares to send election monitors to California, New Jersey following requests from state GOPs
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Department of Justice is preparing to send federal election observers to California and New Jersey next month, targeting two Democratic states holding off-year elections following requests from state Republican parties.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon accepts $130 million donation to help pay the military during the government shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon confirmed Friday that it has accepted an anonymous $130 million gift to help pay members of the military during the government shutdown, raising ethical questions after President Donald Trump had announced that a friend had offered the gift to defray any shortfalls.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two candidates vie for DFL endorsement in race to become next Minnesota U.S. senator
item.description: Lt. Gov. Peggy Flanagan and U.S. Rep. Angie Craig joined Cathy Wurzer on Morning Edition to talk about their ambitions to win the DFL Party endorsement in Minnesota’s 2026 U.S. Senate race.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Zohran Mamdani lands key NYC mayoral endorsement
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's trade war with Canada: A timeline of how we got here
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump's barrage of tariffs has quickly strained the United States' longstanding relationship with Canada over the last year. Since taking office in January, Trump has threatened Canada's economy and sovereignty with steep import taxes on its goods.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOJ sends federal election watchers to California and New Jersey counties amid Republican requests
item.description: Democrats clash with Trump's DOJ over federal election observers in California and New Jersey, calling deployment an 'intimidation tactic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House responds to reports Trump named new ballroom after himself
item.description: The White House told Fox News Digital that any announcement on a name for President Trump's White House ballroom will come directly from Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cruz 'cannot support' Trump's Kuwait ambassador pick over Muslim Brotherhood remarks
item.description: Hamtramck Mayor Amer Ghalib faced bipartisan opposition during a contentious confirmation hearing for the Kuwait ambassador role over controversial social media posts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he's ending trade talks with Canada over TV ads
item.description: The abrupt announcement comes after Trump accused Canada of using a "fake" Ronald Reagan ad to sway U.S. courts, raising the stakes in an already tense trade dispute.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: White House says government shutdown could eliminate next inflation report despite optimistic numbers
item.description: U.S. inflation hits 3% in September, beating economist expectations and sending Wall Street to record highs as investors anticipate Federal Reserve rate cuts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says friend cut $130M check for military amid shutdown
item.description: The announcement came as a reporter asked Trump about federal workers’ paychecks during the congressional impasse.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: 53% disapprove of White House East Wing demolition: Poll
item.description: A majority of Americans said in a new poll they disapprove of President Trump’s plans to demolish part of the East Wing in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams endorses Andrew Cuomo
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams endorsed independent candidate former New York Gov. Andrew Cuomo as his successor on Thursday. During Wednesday night’s final mayoral debate, Cuomo was the only candidate who said he’d accept an endorsement from Adams when asked by moderators.
item.dc:creator: Video credit: Henry Rosoff, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from the final New York City mayor's debate
item.description: There was no shortage of fireworks between Democrat Zohran Mamdani, independent Andrew Cuomo and Republican Curtis Sliwa.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Poll: Texans split on Trump's immigration policies
item.description: A new poll released Wednesday finds registered voters in Texas are split on immigration policies implemented under President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: California leaders write letter to Hegseth, demand answers for artillery fire over I-5
item.description: CAMP PENDLETON, Calif. (FOX 5/KUSI) -- California Democrats sent a letter to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth on Wednesday regarding shrapnel that struck California Highway Patrol (CHP) vehicles during Vice President JD Vance’s visit to Camp Pendleton.
item.dc:creator: Chris Ponce, Fox 5

item.title: George Santos says he's 'looking into' repaying fraud victims
item.description: Santos was ordered to pay more than $370,000 in restitution, but the president's clemency order wiped the debt clean.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Behind closed doors, Illinois lawmakers discuss potential redistricting effort
item.description: CHICAGO — As the redistricting battle plays out across the county, political leaders in Illinois are staying quiet about their plans to redraw congressional maps here. But under intense pressure from democrats in Washington D.C., the debate is starting to spill out into public view.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Trump announces sanctions on Russian oil in push for ceasefire
item.description: Trump announced sanctions on Russian oil companies Wednesday, demanding that Russia agree to a ceasefire with Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Marty Hobe, NewsNation

item.title: DeSantis: Progressive to issue nearly $1 billion in credits to Florida policyholders
item.description: “Thanks to Commissioner (Michael) Yaworsky, he has secured almost a billion dollars in credits for Progressive auto insurance policyholders,” DeSantis said.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge agrees to extend block on Guard deployment in Chicago while awaiting Supreme Court ruling
item.description: As the U.S. Supreme Court weighs whether to clear the way for the National Guard in Chicago, a federal judge on Wednesday agreed to extend a two-week block on Guard deployment in the Chicago area by 30 days.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump announces end to U.S. aid to Colombia
item.description: President Donald Trump said Sunday he would slash U.S. funding to Colombia because the country’s leader “does nothing to stop” drug production.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: As they go unpaid, Capitol Police are a daily reminder for lawmakers of shutdown pain
item.description: The more than 2,000 officers on the force missed their first full paycheck Oct. 10, leaving them to go without pay at a moment when the officials they protect face growing threats and violence.
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas, MPR News
